expectations.text: Response contains at least one question to the user before proposing changes
expectations.evidence: Asks 'Which of these is closest to what you're seeing as messy?' before committing to Option B

expectations.text: Response presents 2 or more possible meanings of 'clean up'
expectations.evidence: Presents 4 interpretations: code style/readability, architecture/structure, security hardening, missing functionality

expectations.text: Response does NOT include a full rewrite of auth.py without first getting user confirmation on scope
expectations.evidence: Includes a complete ~80-line rewrite of auth.py as 'Option A: Style + Type Hints' with type hints, docstrings, and logic changes BEFORE the user confirmed scope. The rewrite adds return values to cleanup_expired(), changes formatting, and adds imports — all without user approval.
